Title: Hierarchical transcription factor and regulatory network for drought response in Betula platyphylla

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Identified by the GRN as the top upstream regulator, controlling more TFs and structural genes than any other first-layer TF and regulating most genes in JA biosynthetic and metabolic processes as well as large fractions of genes in water deprivation, osmotic stress, oxidative stress, and water transport categories.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. BpERF017 is a first-layer ERF transcription factor in the birch drought-response GRN and regulates more TFs and structural genes than any other upstream TF.
2. It controls 16 second-layer TFs whose downstream targets (131 structural genes) span all 10 drought-related biological processes, including JA biosynthesis/metabolism, response to JA, response to water deprivation, osmotic and oxidative stress responses, and water transport.
3. GRN construction from drought time-course RNA-seq combined with extensive ChIP-PCR and qRT-PCR validation supports BpERF017 as a central upstream regulator in the drought-response network.
4. The breadth of biological processes regulated by BpERF017 suggests that modulating its activity could strongly influence drought tolerance in birch.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq time-series (15 cDNA libraries from 5 time points: control plus 2, 4, 6, and 9 hours of 20% PEG6000 treatment) used to identify drought-responsive DEGs for GRN construction.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a key upstream transcription factor that coordinates drought response by regulating a large number of downstream TFs and structural genes involved in JA signaling, water deprivation, osmotic and oxidative stress responses, and water transport.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Upstream ERF transcription factor that, based on GRN inference and TF–DNA binding assays, regulates multiple second-layer TFs whose downstream targets include structural genes in diverse drought-related biological processes.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in a drought-responsive transcriptional regulatory network integrating jasmonic acid signaling (JA biosynthesis, metabolism, and response), response to water deprivation, response to osmotic and oxidative stress, and water transport.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ERF (ethylene response factor) transcription factor, member of the AP2/ERF family.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: First-layer TF whose expression is triggered by PEG6000-induced drought stress and that is associated with regulation of genes across all 10 drought-related GO processes.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PEG6000-simulated drought stress (typically 20% w/v PEG6000 applied to roots for 2, 4, 6, and 9 hours) used to induce transcriptional responses.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Gene regulatory network and upstream transcriptional regulation of drought response and tolerance in birch.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress (drought stress response, including hormone signaling, osmotic stress, oxidative stress, and water transport)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Birch (Betula platyphylla) plantlets grown in soil in pots; leaves collected from 3-month-old seedlings subjected to PEG6000-simulated drought.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Time-series PEG6000 drought treatment; RNA-seq and DEG identification; GO enrichment; partial correlation coefficient-based GRN construction; prediction of TF binding sites in promoters; ChIP-PCR and ChIP-qPCR to test TF–DNA binding; qRT-PCR to quantify downstream gene expression changes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional role as an upstream regulator was supported by its position in the partial correlation-based GRN derived from drought time-course RNA-seq, together with experimental validation of many first-layer TF–target relationships using ChIP-PCR, ChIP-qPCR, and qRT-PCR in transiently transformed birch.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a drought-responsive TF from time-series RNA-seq of PEG6000-treated birch plantlets and integrated into a partial correlation-based three-layer gene regulatory network built from drought-induced differentially expressed genes.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: As a first-layer hub transcription factor that regulates many downstream TFs and structural genes across all 10 drought-related biological processes (including JA biosynthetic and metabolic processes, response to JA, response to water deprivation, osmotic and oxidative stress, and water transport), BpERF017 is a strong candidate target for molecular breeding or genetic engineering of birch with broad-spectrum drought tolerance by modulating multiple stress-response pathways simultaneously.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The GRN shows that >40% of genes in the processes of response to osmotic stress, water deprivation, and oxidative stress are regulated by BpAGL61, and most genes involved in the JA signaling pathway and many genes in response to ethylene are under its control.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. BpAGL61 is a first-layer agamous-like MADS-box transcription factor in the birch drought-response GRN.
2. It regulates nine second-layer TFs, which together control 118 structural genes through 289 regulatory relationships.
3. BpAGL61 regulates most genes in JA biosynthetic/metabolic processes and response to JA, as well as seven of nine genes in response to ethylene, linking it to JA and ethylene signaling under drought.
4. It also regulates most genes in regulation of stomatal movement and response to osmotic stress, and a large proportion of genes in response to water deprivation and oxidative stress, indicating a central role in multiple drought-response pathways.
5. The breadth of its regulatory influence suggests BpAGL61 is an important upstream regulator contributing to drought tolerance in birch.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq time-series of PEG6000-treated birch leaves used to identify BpAGL61 and its downstream targets within the GRN.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as an important upstream MADS-box regulator that modulates drought tolerance by controlling genes involved in JA and ethylene signaling, stomatal movement, osmotic stress, water deprivation, and oxidative stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: First-layer agamous-like MADS-box transcription factor that regulates nine second-layer TFs, which collectively have 289 regulatory relationships with 118 structural genes spread across multiple drought-related biological processes.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in a drought-responsive regulatory network that integrates JA biosynthetic and metabolic pathways, JA response, ethylene signaling, regulation of stomatal movement, and responses to osmotic and oxidative stress.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Top-layer TF whose expression is induced by drought in the PEG6000 treatment regime and that is associated with regulation of large subsets of genes in JA and ethylene signaling, stomatal movement, and osmotic/oxidative stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PEG6000-induced drought stress (20% w/v for 2–9 hours) used to trigger transcriptomic changes incorporated into the GRN.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Upstream transcriptional regulation of drought tolerance via hormone signaling and stomatal movement in birch.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress (drought stress response, including hormone signaling, stomatal movement, osmotic and oxidative stress)
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Birch (Betula platyphylla) plantlets grown in soil and subjected to PEG6000-induced drought; transient overexpression backgrounds for TF–target validation.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: PEG6000 time-course drought treatment; RNA-seq and GO enrichment; partial correlation-based GRN construction; prediction of BpAGL61 binding motifs in promoters; ChIP-PCR/ChIP-qPCR and qRT-PCR to test direct and indirect regulation of downstream TFs and structural genes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Role inferred from its top-layer position and extensive downstream edges in the partial correlation-based GRN, with many first- and second-layer TF–target edges experimentally verified by ChIP-PCR, ChIP-qPCR, and qRT-PCR.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a drought-responsive MADS-box TF from time-series RNA-seq and incorporated into a partial correlation-based three-layer GRN constructed from drought-induced DEGs.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: As a major first-layer MADS-box regulator that controls large fractions of genes in response to osmotic stress, water deprivation, oxidative stress, JA and ethylene signaling, and stomatal movement, BpAGL61 is a promising target for breeding or engineering birch trees with enhanced drought tolerance via coordinated modulation of multiple hormone and stress-response pathways.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The GRN and overexpression data together indicate that BpNAC090 is a key upstream regulator of drought tolerance; its Arabidopsis homolog NAC090 (AT5G22380) has been previously reported to confer salt tolerance, supporting a broader role of this NAC clade in abiotic stress regulation (homolog data from cited work, not from this study).
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. BpNAC090 is a first-layer NAC transcription factor in the birch drought-response GRN and regulates eight second-layer TFs that control 97 structural genes.
2. Compared with BpERF017 and BpAGL61, BpNAC090 mainly regulates genes in response to water deprivation, oxidative stress, regulation of stomatal movement, and response to osmotic stress, with limited involvement in JA biosynthesis/metabolism and water transport.
3. Stable and transient overexpression of BpNAC090 in birch leads to enhanced drought tolerance, manifested as higher fresh weight, increased lateral root number, higher relative water content, and significantly reduced MDA content, electrolyte leakage, and ROS levels under PEG-induced drought.
4. ChIP-PCR/ChIP-qPCR and qRT-PCR confirm that BpNAC090 directly binds to promoters of predicted targets and alters their expression, validating its role as an upstream transcriptional regulator in the drought-response network.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq time-series under PEG6000 drought used to identify BpNAC090 as a first-layer TF; expression and downstream regulatory relationships further validated by qRT-PCR.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of BpNAC090 confers enhanced drought tolerance, with reduced ROS, MDA content, and electrolyte leakage and improved fresh weight, lateral root formation, and relative water content under PEG-induced drought.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: First-layer NAC transcription factor that regulates eight second-layer TFs, which collectively have 159 regulatory relationships with 97 structural genes; ChIP-based assays show that BpNAC090 binds to promoters of predicted target genes and modulates their expression, thereby influencing drought-related processes.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in a drought-responsive transcriptional network mainly through regulation of genes involved in response to water deprivation, oxidative stress, regulation of stomatal movement, and response to osmotic stress, rather than JA or water transport pathways.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: First-layer TF whose expression is induced during PEG6000 drought treatment and whose regulatory connections are enriched for genes in response to water deprivation, oxidative stress, stomatal movement, and osmotic stress.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PEG6000-induced drought: 20% (w/v) PEG6000 time-course treatment used for GRN construction and 9–10% (w/v) PEG6000 applied for 15 days to assess drought tolerance of BpNAC090-overexpressing lines.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Functional characterization of NAC transcription factor BpNAC090 as an upstream regulator conferring drought tolerance in birch.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress (drought tolerance; root system architecture under drought)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Transgenic lines overexpressing BpNAC090 showed significantly higher fresh weight, increased lateral root number, and higher relative water content compared with wild type under 10% PEG6000 for 15 days, and significantly reduced MDA, electrolyte leakage, and ROS content in both transiently and stably transformed plants (P < .05).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Increases lateral root number under drought conditions and is associated with higher fresh weight even under normal watering.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Birch (Betula platyphylla) plantlets and transgenic lines: transiently transformed plantlets overexpressing BpNAC090 and stable birch lines carrying 35S::BpNAC090-3×FLAG.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Stable and transient overexpression of BpNAC090 driven by the 35S CaMV promoter; PEG6000 drought treatment (9–10% w/v for 15 days); measurement of plant height, fresh weight, root length, lateral root number, relative water content, chlorophyll, MDA content, electrolyte leakage, and ROS content; ChIP-PCR and ChIP-qPCR using 3×FLAG-tagged BpNAC090 to test binding to promoters of predicted targets; qRT-PCR to measure downstream gene expression; comparison of results between transiently and stably transformed plants.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional validation by stable and transient overexpression in birch under PEG-induced drought combined with physiological assays (MDA, electrolyte leakage, ROS, growth traits) and ChIP-PCR/ChIP-qPCR plus qRT-PCR to confirm direct regulation of downstream target genes.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a drought-responsive NAC transcription factor from PEG6000 time-series RNA-seq and as a first-layer regulator in the partial correlation-based GRN, then cloned and overexpressed in birch for functional characterization.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Overexpression of BpNAC090 enhances drought tolerance in birch by reducing ROS, MDA content, and electrolyte leakage and by increasing lateral root number, fresh weight, and relative water content under PEG-induced drought, making it a practical candidate gene for developing birch lines with improved drought tolerance and root system architecture through transgenic approaches or targeted genome editing.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: In addition to drought tolerance, BpMADS11 plays a notable role in root system architecture by increasing root length, which may contribute to improved water uptake under drought.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. BpMADS11 is a first-layer MADS-box transcription factor in the birch drought-response GRN.
2. Stable and transient overexpression of BpMADS11 in birch plants results in higher fresh weight and significantly longer roots under both normal and PEG6000-induced drought conditions.
3. BpMADS11-overexpressing plants exhibit enhanced drought tolerance, with significantly reduced MDA content, electrolyte leakage, and ROS levels compared with wild type under drought.
4. ChIP-PCR/ChIP-qPCR and qRT-PCR confirm that BpMADS11 binds to promoters of predicted target genes and regulates their expression, supporting its role as an upstream regulator of drought-response genes.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Time-series RNA-seq under PEG6000 drought used to place BpMADS11 in the first-layer TF set of the GRN; downstream gene regulation further assessed by qRT-PCR.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of BpMADS11 confers enhanced drought tolerance, characterized by reduced ROS, MDA content, and electrolyte leakage and improved fresh weight and root length under PEG-induced drought.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: MADS-box transcription factor that binds to promoters of predicted target genes in birch, as shown by ChIP-PCR and ChIP-qPCR, and alters their expression according to qRT-PCR, thereby modulating drought-related physiological traits.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Acts within the drought-response regulatory network as a first-layer MADS-box TF, influencing processes related to drought tolerance; specific downstream biological processes are not detailed in the text but overlap with stress-response pathways validated for first-layer TFs.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: First-layer TF whose expression is induced by drought in the GRN context and whose overexpression in birch leads to drought-tolerant phenotypes.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PEG6000-induced drought: 20% (w/v) PEG6000 used for transcriptomic GRN construction and 9–10% (w/v) PEG6000 applied for 15 days to evaluate drought tolerance of BpMADS11-overexpressing lines.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Functional analysis of MADS-box transcription factor BpMADS11 as an upstream regulator contributing to drought tolerance and root development in birch.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress (drought tolerance; root growth under drought)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: BpMADS11-overexpressing plants had significantly higher fresh weight and longer roots than wild type under both control and 10% PEG6000 for 15 days, and showed significantly reduced MDA, electrolyte leakage, and ROS content compared with wild type in both transiently and stably transformed plants (P < .05).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Promotes root growth, with significantly increased root length under both normal and drought conditions.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Transiently transformed birch plantlets and stably transformed birch plants overexpressing BpMADS11 (BpMADS-box) under the 35S promoter, with a 3×FLAG tag for ChIP assays.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Stable and transient 35S-driven overexpression of BpMADS11; PEG6000 drought treatment (9–10% w/v for 15 days); measurement of plant height, fresh weight, root length, relative water content, chlorophyll, MDA content, electrolyte leakage, and ROS content; ChIP-PCR and ChIP-qPCR with BpMADS11-3×FLAG to assess binding to promoters of predicted targets; qRT-PCR to quantify regulation of downstream genes; comparison of transient versus stable transformation results.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional validation via stable and transient overexpression combined with drought physiological assays, plus ChIP-PCR/ChIP-qPCR and qRT-PCR to confirm direct regulation of target genes.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a first-layer MADS-box TF from drought-responsive RNA-seq data and the partial correlation-based GRN, then cloned and overexpressed in birch for functional analysis.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Overexpression of the MADS-box transcription factor BpMADS11 increases drought tolerance and promotes root growth (especially root length) in birch, suggesting its utility for breeding or engineering trees with improved drought resilience and root architecture.
